reference.title: Overlay Management Tunnel Configuration Example
reference.shortdesc: This section contains Cloud-Native Router MPLSoUDP tunnel configuration for the overlay management example.

section.title: Managed Node Tunnel Configuration Example
section.p: This configuration creates an MPLSoUDP tunnel endpoint and VRF to route OAM traffic. Put this configuration into a configlet and apply on the managed node, which we call node1 below.
section.draft-comment: I changed all 11.11.11.11 to 10.11.11.11 and all 12.12.12.12 to 10.12.12.12 to align with doc rules (IP addresses must be in private range). Also changed the IPv6 addresses to 2001:db8:x for same reason.
section.codeblock: set groups node1 system processes routing bgp tcp-listen-port 178 set groups node1 interfaces ens1f0v2 unit 0 family inet address 192.168.1.1/24 set groups node1 interfaces ens1f0v1 unit 0 family inet address 10.10.1.1/24 set groups node1 interfaces ens1f0v1 unit 0 family inet6 address 2001:db8:10:10:1::1/65 set groups node1 interfaces lo0 unit 0 family inet address 10.11.11.11/32 set groups node1 interfaces lo0 unit 0 family iso address 49.0002.0190.0160.0023.00 set groups node1 policy-options policy-statement pplb then load-balance per-packet set groups node1 routing-instances green instance-type vrf set groups node1 routing-instances green interface ens1f0v1 set groups node1 routing-instances green route-distinguisher 40:40 set groups node1 routing-instances green vrf-target target:40:40 set groups node1 routing-options route-distinguisher-id 10.11.11.11 set groups node1 routing-options router-id 10.11.11.11 set groups node1 routing-options dynamic-tunnels tun1 source-address 10.11.11.11 set groups node1 routing-options dynamic-tunnels tun1 udp set groups node1 routing-options dynamic-tunnels tun1 destination-networks 10.12.12.12/32 set groups node1 routing-options forwarding-table export pplb set groups node1 routing-options forwarding-table channel vrouter export pplb set groups node1 protocols bgp tcp-connect-port 178 set groups node1 protocols bgp group node1 type internal set groups node1 protocols bgp group node1 local-address 10.11.11.11 set groups node1 protocols bgp group node1 family inet-vpn unicast set groups node1 protocols bgp group node1 local-as 64512 set groups node1 protocols bgp group node1 neighbor 10.12.12.12 set groups node1 protocols isis interface ens1f0v2 set groups node1 protocols isis interface lo0.0 set groups node1 protocols isis level 1 disable set groups node1 protocols isis level 2 enable set apply-groups base set apply-groups internal set apply-groups node1 set groups node1 routing-instances green vrf-table-label

section.title: Transit Node Tunnel Configuration Example
section.p: This configuration creates an MPLSoUDP tunnel endpoint and VRF to route OAM traffic. Put this configuration into a configlet and apply on the transit node, which we call node2 below.
section.codeblock: set groups node2 system processes routing bgp tcp-listen-port 178 set groups node2 interfaces ens1f0 unit 0 family inet address 192.168.1.2/24 set groups node2 interfaces ens1f1 unit 0 family inet address 10.10.2.1/24 set groups node2 interfaces ens1f1 unit 0 family inet6 address 2001:db8:10:10:2::1/65 set groups node2 interfaces lo0 unit 0 family inet address 10.12.12.12/32 set groups node2 interfaces lo0 unit 0 family iso address 49.0002.0190.0160.0024.00 set groups node2 policy-options policy-statement pplb then load-balance per-packet set groups node2 routing-instances green instance-type vrf set groups node2 routing-instances green interface ens1f1 set groups node2 routing-instances green route-distinguisher 40:40 set groups node2 routing-instances green vrf-target target:40:40 set groups node2 routing-options route-distinguisher-id 10.12.12.12 set groups node2 routing-options router-id 10.12.12.12 set groups node2 routing-options dynamic-tunnels tun1 source-address 10.12.12.12 set groups node2 routing-options dynamic-tunnels tun1 udp set groups node2 routing-options dynamic-tunnels tun1 destination-networks 10.11.11.11/32 set groups node2 routing-options forwarding-table export pplb set groups node2 routing-options forwarding-table channel vrouter export pplb set groups node2 protocols bgp tcp-connect-port 178 set groups node2 protocols bgp group node1 type internal set groups node2 protocols bgp group node1 local-address 10.12.12.12 set groups node2 protocols bgp group node1 family inet-vpn unicast set groups node2 protocols bgp group node1 local-as 64512 set groups node2 protocols bgp group node1 neighbor 10.11.11.11 set groups node2 protocols isis interface ens1f0 set groups node2 protocols isis interface lo0.0 set groups node2 protocols isis level 1 disable set groups node2 protocols isis level 2 enable set apply-groups node2 set groups node2 routing-instances green vrf-table-label
